relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The command is given for lived practice in the land.
Reverence is carried through children and grandchildren.
Listening becomes well-being and multiplication.
Hear, Israel: YHWH is our Divine, YHWH is one.
Love gathers heart, soul, and every capacity into covenant.
The words are placed upon the heart before they are placed anywhere else.
They are taught through sitting, walking, lying down, and rising.
Hand and sight are marked by remembrance.
Home and gate become thresholds of Torah.
The people will enter cities they did not build.
They will receive houses, cisterns, vineyards, and trees shaped by others' labor.
Satisfaction is where forgetting becomes dangerous.
Service belongs to the Liberator alone.
The surrounding gods may not replace covenant.
Jealous fire guards the relationship from divided allegiance.
YHWH is not to be tested as at Massah.
Testimony and command are guarded diligently.
The path seeks what is right and good.
The land promise remains entangled with expulsion of enemies.
A child's question opens the door to memory.
The answer begins: we were slaves, and YHWH brought us out.
Liberation is remembered through signs against imperial power.
The people are brought out in order to be brought in.
Commandment is framed as life and good.
Righteousness becomes faithful practice before YHWH.

contemplative_note: The Shema is not an abstract slogan. It moves from hearing to loving, teaching, binding, writing, remembering, and answering a child's question. Its center is one living sentence: we were slaves, and YHWH brought us out.
